resources.string: Customize the display of the different pages of the application
Choose operators, sources, and display order for photos and speedtests.
Manage the order and display of external shortcuts
Choose the elements to include on the image
Configure your main operator to make it easier to use the measurement tools on the map.
Which map provider do you prefer?
Welcome to GeoTower, the app for locating nearby cell towers and getting information about them.
Quickly list the nearest radio supports and sites around your position.
Browse antennas, filters, layers and technical information directly on a map.
Access frequencies, azimuths, photos, profiles and measurement tools when data is available.
Please allow location permission so GeoTower can quickly locate nearby transmitters.
Your position is used to sort antennas around you and open the right area immediately.
The location button can center the map, then follow your movement when you enable it.
You can continue without permission and change it later from Android settings.
Please allow notification permission to show notifications for database downloads, offline map downloads, and new database availability.
GeoTower can show progress for database and offline map downloads while they are running.
You can be notified when a new database is available without opening the app to check.
No social notifications or ads: only long-running operations and important data updates.
Enable a discreet live tracker to keep the nearest antenna for your operator visible in a notification.
Live tracking uses the default operator you just chose to show relevant information.
The notification can update while you move to show the nearest site.
You can turn live notifications off at any time from GeoTower settings.
Location permission was denied. Some features will be limited, such as nearby search, map recentering, and live tracking.
Notification permission was denied. Some features will be limited, such as download progress, update alerts, and live notifications.
You have not chosen a default operator. The filtering tools on the map will be disabled.\n\nDo you really want to continue?
You have not downloaded the app database, so no items will be displayed on screen.
Are you sure you want to continue?
The offline database has been successfully installed. The application is ready to run at full speed.
Choose the style that suits you.
Download the entire database to use the list offline. Warning: large file.
Enable or disable blur (consumes more battery)
Are you sure you want to restore default settings? This will delete all settings you have made in the app.
Show one category at a time
Display support details and site details individually in full screen
Split display for compatible screens: the context stays on the left while details open on the right.
File size: %1$.1f MB\nWi-Fi download is recommended.
Are you sure you want to delete the database?
Download failed. Please check your connection.
Map %1$s is ready offline. Tap to open.
An antenna update is available. Tap to open the download section.
GeoTower %1$s is available. Tap to open the download.
%1$d/%2$d photos sent successfully to Signal Quest!
%1$d/%2$d photos sent to Signal Quest. Some failed.
%1$d out of %2$d photo(s) successfully sent to Signal Quest.
Site %1$s is not in the displayed area. Move the map to its city first.
Add at least one photo before uploading.
SignalQuest upload is disabled for this operator.
A photo exceeds the 20 MB limit.
GeoTower is connected to Android Auto.
No site found around your position.
Allow location in GeoTower on the phone.
GeoTower needs location to show sites around you.
Tap here, go to Permissions > Location, then select "Allow all the time".
GeoTower allows you to locate cell towers around you and identify available technologies.
Chooses the artwork used on Home, splash and About.
Allow "Always" for the widget to refresh
Enable or disable blur (consumes more battery)
Automatically uses the phone system colors (Material You).
Dynamic mode (wallpaper) takes priority. Otherwise, choose a native Material 3 palette.
Show only if sources above have no photos
Choose operators, photo sources, and display order.
Choose operators and sources used for best speedtests.
Choose operators, sources, and display order for photos and speedtests.
You are offline.\nCommunity photos cannot be retrieved without an internet connection. The diagram remains visible when available.
Activation date not specified by ANFR
The offline database has been successfully installed. The application is ready to run at full speed.
You haven\'t downloaded the app\'s database, so you won\'t have any items displayed on the screen.
Are you sure you want to continue?
Choose the elements to include on the image
Do you really want to delete all downloaded maps?
Are you sure you want to delete the database?
Developed by Julien and GitHub contributors 😉
Display support details and site details individually in full screen
Split display for compatible screens: the context stays on the left while details open on the right.
Do not show this message again
Terrain and Fresnel zone analysis in progress.

The Fresnel zone is the volume around the radio path. For a more reliable link, at least 60%% of this zone is usually kept clear, so terrain can affect the signal even if it does not exactly cut the direct path.
Terrain may cut the direct path
Terrain does not cut the direct path between you and the panel
User location unavailable. Enable location and try again.
Site not found for this profile.
The calculation uses IGN elevation data and requires an internet connection. Try again when the network is available.
The calculation uses IGN elevation data and requires an internet connection. You can temporarily save your current position to run the calculation automatically when the network returns.
Your current position and this site are temporarily saved. The elevation profile will be calculated automatically when the connection returns.
You are offline. A previously calculated elevation profile exists for this site. Do you want to display it?
Panel height for the selected frequency.
Manage the order and display of external shortcuts
Find the table of contents, screen-by-screen explanations, search codes and button meanings here.
No help topic matches this search.
The local database is incompatible. Download a valid database to continue.
Your MaterialUi color is too light. For better readability, dark blue has been applied.
Follows the active app icon and theme.
Do you really want to delete this map from your device?
Which map provider do you prefer?
Offline vector maps and render theme (Elevate).
You must keep at least one frequency.
You must keep at least one mobile technology (2G, 3G, 4G, or 5G).
Download the database to use the app.
Show one category at a time
Searches the full ANFR address of the site.
Searches for sites in the city using the Nominatim area, like the map.
Download the entire database to use the list offline. Warning : large file.
Download maps of France to navigate without an internet connection.
You can turn live notifications off at any time from GeoTower settings.
Enable a discreet live tracker to keep the nearest antenna for your operator visible in a notification.
The notification can update while you move to show the nearest site.
Live tracking uses the default operator you just chose to show relevant information.
Please allow location permission so GeoTower can quickly locate nearby transmitters.
Location permission was denied. Some features will be limited, such as nearby search, map recentering, and live tracking.
The location button can center the map, then follow your movement when you enable it.
Your position is used to sort antennas around you and open the right area immediately.
You can continue without permission and change it later from Android settings.
No social notifications or ads: only long-running operations and important data updates.
Please allow notification permission to show notifications for database downloads, offline map downloads, and new database availability.
Notification permission was denied. Some features will be limited, such as download progress, update alerts, and live notifications.
GeoTower can show progress for database and offline map downloads while they are running.
You can be notified when a new database is available without opening the app to check.
Welcome to GeoTower, the app for locating nearby cell towers and getting information about them.
Browse antennas, filters, layers and technical information directly on a map.
Quickly list the nearest radio supports and sites around your position.
Access frequencies, azimuths, photos, profiles and measurement tools when data is available.
Customize the display of the different pages of the application
Configure your main operator to make it easier to use the measurement tools on the map.
Added a setting to show or hide EXIF information
Improved multilingual handling across the app
Added new translations for notifications, the widget and Android Auto
Site photos now display with rounded corners
Operator logos look better when no site photo is available
Statistics are available as soon as local data is ready
Added support details by frequency band
Charts are easier to read, animated and easier to browse
More complete external photo upload history
More flexible management of some app features
Added a home announcement banner in the selected language
Better handling of temporarily unavailable pages
Release notes and update notifications are better localized
Redesigned statistics with declared and active sites
More customizable display, with details by frequency
Added quick access to settings from several app screens
Better handling of CellularFR photos and links based on availability
Added a page to view all speedtests
More complete sorting, filters and details for each result
The “Best speedtest” card is now clickable
The best result follows the criterion chosen in settings
Added a new mini-map antenna widget
Displays nearby antennas, azimuths and offline sites
Open the map directly from the widget
More compact and easier to read display
Improved tracking icon based on distance
Better highlight of the site photo
More flexible management of some features depending on app availability
Added new settings for photo and speedtest sources
Photo display is better synchronized between Support and Site pages
Photos are filtered by enabled operator and sources
Added checks to make uploads more reliable
SignalQuest connection is simpler and better integrated into the app
SignalQuest photos, uploads and speedtests are easier to access
Live photos are available without a local SignalQuest setting
Complete improvement of language handling across the app
Expanded translations in French, English, Portuguese, Italian, German and Spanish
Better translation of screens, widgets, notifications, Android Auto, help, maps and sharing
More consistent text across the app
Better upload recovery after a failure
Clearer status tracking for each photo
Site photo can now be shown in the notification
Better support for older local databases
More careful cleanup when something goes wrong
Improved translated texts for SignalQuest, help, errors and the throughput calculator
Release notes are now better integrated with translations
Are you sure you want to restore default settings? This will delete all settings you have made in the app.
The app will restart to apply the change.
Estimated radio throughput: excludes network load, real signal, backhaul and exact phone limits.
No speedtest recorded for this site.
National Frequency Agency (ANFR).\nData from Cartoradio (Open Data).
© IGN - National Institute of Geographic and Forest Information.

• © Signal Quest developed by Alexandre Germain
• © RNC Mobile developed by Cédric
• © eNB-Analytics developed by Tristan
• © GeoRadio - The alternative icon comes from the GéoRadio iOS app developed by Hugo Martin.
• Original concept based on the GéoRadio app
• Fun icon designed by Johan
Number of declared 2G sites per operator
Number of declared 3G sites per operator
Number of declared 4G sites per operator
Number of declared 5G sites per operator
Number of declared physical sites per operator
Choose the style that suits you.
Choose a point on the map
Assumption: panel/support height, handset at 1.5 m, typical vertical tilt 4°-8° with a 6° nominal point.
Panel/support height unavailable: unable to estimate the main cone zone.
The circle marks the optimal distance; dots show panel axes where signal should be strongest.
Backhaul represents the link behind the antenna: fiber 100%%, microwave link 84%%, limited 55%% on download.
Environment multiplies the result: outdoor 100%%, vehicle 85%%, indoor 65%%, deep indoor 45%%.
The selected QAM sets the raw radio throughput: higher modulation increases theoretical speed but assumes a better signal.
Network load reduces per-user throughput: unknown 100%%, light 90%%, medium 68%%, heavy 46%%, saturated 28%% on download.
Position adjusts throughput by radio zone: in the beam 106%%, too close 75%%, too far 68%%, outside azimuth 45%%.
These values weight the estimate: good SINR allows more efficient modulation, while weak RSRP reduces stability.
These choices adjust cell load, backhaul quality and the number of counted 4G carriers.
The result is a theoretical radio throughput: it does not account for distance, signal level, SINR, network load, backhaul, or phone limits.
No usable 4G or 5G band was detected for this site.
Site not found for this calculation.
Panel azimuth unavailable: only the distance cone can be used.
Radio cone unavailable: missing panel/support height.
Current position used for the analysis.
Map point used for the analysis.
No position selected: the calculation keeps a neutral position coefficient.
Location permission denied: choose a point on the map or try again after enabling it.
Current position is unavailable for now.
Upload is weighted for a handset: lower transmit power, less MIMO and usually lower modulation than download.
Move/zoom the mini map if needed, then tap the point to analyze.
The total spectrum may be inaccurate due to likely declaration errors.
Get alerted when a new database or APK is available
Some photos could not be sent (network issue).
Local thumbnails and history rows will be deleted. Photos already sent to external apps are not changed.
No photo upload recorded on this device.
Add a description for this batch (optional)...
MIMO and modulation are not published at site level, so the %1$s profile is applied.
Band %1$s excluded: operator allocation not found.
Band %1$s may be shared between 4G and 5G, so its throughput is not fully added.
Estimated radio cone: nominal point %1$s, useful zone %2$s-%3$s from panel/support height.
RSRP accounts for 40%% of the radio score and SNR/SINR for 60%%. The current coefficient gives 4G %1$d%% / 5G %2$d%%.
The total keeps the best %1$d 4G carrier(s) for the profile. Low bands 700/800/900 MHz are not added together.
Conservative profile: average modulation, upload strongly limited by handset transmit power.
Ideal profile: plausible very good radio conditions, but upload remains capped on the handset side.
Custom profile: DL/UL modulations are manually tuned, with upload still limited like a handset.
Standard profile: 4G MIMO 2x2 and 5G MIMO 4x4 for download, upload calculated like a real handset.
Operator not recognized for Arcep allocations
Network load, backhaul and the phone\'s exact capabilities are not known.
Upload throughput is limited to the two best aggregated frequencies, which is a more realistic assumption for mobile networks in France.
4G aggregation between low bands 700/800/900 MHz is limited: many phones do not combine these carriers.
Selected 4G aggregation limit: only the best carriers are counted.
Profile 5G aggregation limit: only the best carriers are counted.
No compatible Arcep allocation for metropolitan France was found for this technology and band.
Band potentially shared between 4G and 5G: it is not counted twice.
ANFR/data.gouv for declared frequencies, Arcep for operator allocations, ETSI/3GPP TS 38.306 and TS 36.306/36.213 for the radio model.
ANFR/data.gouv for declared frequencies, Arcep for operator allocations, 3GPP for the radio model.
Conservative profile: 4G 64-QAM downlink, 16-QAM uplink, 5G NR 64-QAM, limited aggregation and DSS not counted twice.
Standard profile: 4G 256-QAM downlink with 2x2 MIMO, 64-QAM phone-side uplink, 5G n78 256-QAM downlink with 4x4 MIMO, 64-QAM uplink on 2 layers, DSS not counted twice.
Ideal profile: plausible very good radio conditions, 4G downlink with 4x4 MIMO, 5G NR 256-QAM, more open aggregation and no DSS double counting.
Custom profile: downlink and uplink modulations chosen in the interface, with upload treated like a phone.
The recommended path to set up GeoTower and quickly find an antenna.
Explains the main buttons, database banner and Help button.
Search by city, address, postal code, support, ANFR, coordinates, type and technology.
Markers, search, filters, GPS position, layers and map tools.
Orientation toward antennas and understanding bearings around your position.
Everything about the tower, roof, operators and linked sites.
Frequencies, azimuths, height, status, photos, links, sharing and advanced tools.
Terrain, line of sight, Fresnel zone, recalculation and network limits.
Conservative/ideal/custom modes, bands, modulations, MIMO and optimal distance.
Carousel, full screen, SignalQuest upload, descriptions and target operator.
Image options, support, frequencies, dates, speedtest, throughput and elevation profile.
Appearance, One UI, units, split screen, customization, database, offline maps and notifications.
Downloads, progress notifications, updates and local storage.
Version, data sources, development information and useful links.
Meaning of common icons and solutions to frequent issues.
On first launch, make sure the database is downloaded, location is allowed and your main operator is set in settings. Without the local database, antenna screens cannot show ANFR sites.
From Home, open Nearby antennas for a list around you or Antenna map to explore visually. Both paths then lead to support details and site details.
A support is the tower, roof or building. A site is an operator installation on that support. Technical details, frequencies, azimuths and advanced tools are mostly on the site detail page.
Home groups the app\'s main shortcuts. Some buttons can be hidden or moved from Settings > Page customization.
If the database is missing, outdated or downloading, a banner appears at the top. If the network is unavailable, an offline banner warns that some features cannot update.
Search accepts free text and codes. For a city, GeoTower can use the same logic as the map: find the municipality area, then filter sites across the full ANFR address.

Use prefixes when a search may be ambiguous: ville:Paris, cp:75015, gps:48.8566,2.3522, support:123456, anfr:987654, tech:5G, type:pylon, op:Orange. Accents, uppercase letters and hyphens are normalized to make city searches easier.
Each result card shows the support, address, distance and available operators. Tapping opens the detail page. In split screen, the list stays on the left and details open on the right.
The map lets you browse supports and sites. Tapping a marker opens its information. The GPS button recenters on your position when location is available.
The toolbox lets you search a city or address, then refine display by operator, technology, frequency or layer. Filters avoid overloading the map when an area contains many antennas.
If an offline map is downloaded and selected, GeoTower can show the local layer without downloading standard tiles. Files and areas are managed in settings.
The compass uses your position, phone sensors and antenna data to help you understand where nearby sites are located.
The back button closes the screen. Internal actions can start antenna search, stop live tracking or open details depending on the displayed mode.
The support is the physical structure: tower, building, water tower, roof or another type. Several operators and sites can be linked to the same support.
Support detail buttons open the location on the map, start navigation, share information or view community photos.
The site page describes an operator installation: operator banner, support, height, azimuths, frequencies, technologies, activation status and external links.
Action buttons open tools related to the site. Some blocks can be hidden or moved from site page customization.
The elevation profile compares your current position to the selected site. It helps estimate whether terrain may block the signal. The calculation depends on GPS position and online elevation data.

The calculation gives theoretical throughput. Conservative mode lowers expectations, ideal mode shows a favorable scenario, and custom mode lets you adjust bands, technologies and modulations. Upload accounts for the fact that a phone transmits with far less power than an antenna.

The 4G, 5G and project buttons choose which technologies are included. Band checkboxes select frequencies. Modulation sliders choose QPSK, 16 QAM, 64 QAM or 256 QAM depending on the expected radio level.
The carousel shows available community photos. In full screen, controls let you close, switch photos and inspect the image more comfortably.
SignalQuest upload lets you attach images to a support or site. Select files, check the target operator, add a description if needed, then start the upload. Upload requires a network connection.
From a detail page, the Share button opens options. You can include support details, frequencies, activation dates, best speedtest, throughput calculator and sometimes the elevation profile.
Each checkbox adds or removes a block from the final image. The image respects the units chosen in settings, such as distances in kilometers or miles.
Settings group theme, OLED mode, One UI design, language, default operator, metric or imperial units and settings navigation style.
Page mode makes settings more compartmentalized. Split screen shows compatible screens in two panels: context on the left, detail or tool on the right.
This menu is used to show, hide or move page blocks. For Home, you can also enable the Help button and choose its corner.
The database contains ANFR information used by detail pages, the map and searches. Download can be started from Home or Settings. Progress notifications take you to the corresponding section.
Offline maps let you display an area without relying on network tiles. Download an area, select the offline layer, then use the map normally inside the available area.
About shows the GeoTower version, data sources, development information and useful links. The side bar lets you quickly switch sections when the screen is wide enough.
If you come from settings or go back there from About, GeoTower limits unnecessary navigation stacking so you do not need to press Back many times.
The same icons appear across several screens. They generally keep the same role.

If no antenna appears, check the database, filters and search area. If GPS does not respond, check Android permission and wait a few seconds outside or near a window. If the offline map does not appear, make sure the file covers the visible area.
Opens the list of sites around your position or searched area.
Opens the interactive map with markers, filters and map tools.
Opens the orientation tool to aim toward antennas around you.
Opens preferences, customization, offline maps and database management.
Shows version, data sources and development information.
Opens this help center. Its position is configured in Home page customization.
Type a city, address, postal code, support ID, ANFR ID, technology, support type or GPS coordinates.
Clears the search and returns to the previous list.
Inserts common searches. They can be hidden from page customization.
Shows the list of available codes and how to use them.
Adds more results to the list.
Increases the search area when no relevant site is found.
Starts or restarts GPS search and centers the map on your position.
Zooms the map in or out.
Shows the current map orientation if enabled.
Shows an estimate of visible distances.
Updates the target antenna or site based on your position.
Stops active tracking and returns to normal mode.
Centers the GeoTower map on this support.
Opens the phone navigation app toward the coordinates.
Opens sharing and image generation options.
Shows community photos and diagrams if available.
Opens the detail page for an operator installed on the support.
Calculates terrain between your position and the site.
Opens the throughput calculator based on frequencies and radio assumptions.
Opens page block customization when available.
Runs the analysis again with current position and data.
Temporarily saves the request to run it when the connection returns.
Unlocks fine settings for modulation, technologies and bands.
Shows the area where the user is most likely to be inside the antenna emission cone.
Visualizes the optimal distance circle and points where estimated throughput is strongest.
Returns to the previous screen or local contents.
Clears a field or closes a mode.
Starts a database or map download.
Restarts a calculation, check or resets settings depending on context.
Free text or code search field.
Results that open details or split screen.
Map, navigation, sharing and photo actions.
Tools: profile, throughput, sharing and settings.
Route between your position and the site.
Notifications leading to the right section.
You have not chosen a default operator. The filtering tools on the map will be disabled.\n\nDo you really want to continue?
because they require a default operator.
Warning: A high frequency (30 min) may increase background battery consumption.
GeoTower does not collect any personal data. Your settings and favorites are stored only on your device.
